public_functions.behavior_description: Enable and clock the CCU6 module. Configure the CCU6 T12 timer as the base for PWM generation with symmetric (center-aligned) mode at 20 kHz. Initialize the CCU6 high/low-side PWM (PwmHl) configuration for three channels (T12 ch0..2) to drive complementary outputs with dead-time insertion; set active-high polarity for the high-side and active-low for the low-side, and configure the output pads for push-pull with appropriate drive strength. Set dead time for both rising and falling edges to 1 microsecond. Configure a single period-match (T12 PM) interrupt on CPU0 with the requested priority, attach it to the timer associated with the first channel, and leave other channels without interrupts. Initialize the timer and PwmHl drivers with the prepared configurations, install the ISR handler and initialize interrupt routing, then start all channels synchronously so they run in lockstep.

Maintain an array of three duty cycle values, one per phase. On each call: for each phase, check whether adding a fixed STEP value would reach or exceed 100%. If so, wrap that phase's duty to 0.0 before applying the increment. Then increment all three phase duties by STEP. After updating all three values, apply them immediately to the PWM driver using the multi-channel immediate update API so that the new duty cycles take effect coherently across all configured channels while preserving center alignment and configured deadtime.
